title: asclepias subulata
intro: Asclepias subulata, also known as antelope horns or dwarf milkweed, is a perennial plant native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico, characterized by its unique, horn-like seed pods. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can be attributed to its milky latex. Consuming the plant or its preparations may provide various health benefits, such as reducing inflammation, improving wound healing, and supporting the immune system. Asclepias subulata can be prepared in various herbal forms, including teas, tinctures, and salves, which can be used to treat various skin and respiratory issues.
